requirements.notes: Canonical retail-sentiment feed. Covers WSB, X, Reddit, StockTwits aggregation. Wired and continuous from 2024.
Covers all OHLCV-derived signals: RSI, SMA, EMA, ADV, volume z-score, returns. One entry for the underlying feed.

Required for the earnings proximity conviction modifier. Not wired into the Lab. Marked non-core because the dampener can be applied manually via the operator review gate in v1; automated use would require a connector. Flag as v2.

warnings: earnings_calendar: MISSING - Required for the earnings proximity conviction modifier. Not wired into the Lab. Marked non-core because the dampener can be applied manually via the operator review gate in v1; automated use would require a connector. Flag as v2.

operator_message: Not sure if the spec updated. Looks the same

conversation_at_revision.content: I know right now we have our exit set to +20% / -10%, but is there a way to make that rule a bit more dynamic? For instance, if our conviction that the stock is bullish is very high, that could increase our exit percentage, and lower it when our conviction is low. Also ensure we’re taking advantage of all relevant data we have access to. RSI, earnings, recent headlines. Any questions you have for me?

Love the direction — a few quick questions before I revise. (1) For the conviction-scaled exits, should conviction be a continuous score that slides the profit target across a range (e.g. +12% to +28%), or do you prefer discrete tiers (low/medium/high mapped to fixed targets)? (2) On earnings: are you thinking we *avoid* entries near earnings dates (event risk filter), *favor* them (catalyst play), or use earnings proximity as a conviction modifier? (3) For recent headlines, the catalog has no wired news/NLP feed — apewisdom_top100 is our proxy for retail sentiment buzz; should I treat headline sentiment as a manual operator-review input (like the existing market-research gate) rather than an automated signal?

1. I think it should be continuous. 2. Conviction modifier, but need to be careful here, earnings can swing movement in either direction 3. Yeah I guess for now but how easily could we get some news headlines? Any worth free APIs out there?

Got it — continuous conviction scaling on exits, earnings proximity as a conviction modifier (with a dampening penalty for the uncertainty), and headlines stay in the manual gate for now (free news APIs exist but none are wired into the Lab yet; flagging that as a v2 candidate). Here's the revised spec.

talon_reply: Apologies — here's the full revised spec with all three changes applied: (1) continuous conviction score driving dynamic profit targets, (2) earnings proximity as a conviction modifier with an uncertainty dampener, and (3) headlines noted as a v2 candidate in implementation notes.
prompt:
You are Talon's spec-revision mode in the Vires Research Lab.
You are iterating with the operator on an existing StrategySpecV1.
Return only the structured object requested by the schema. Do not invent backtest results.

Output rules:
- Return kind="clarification" + a brief reply (under 3 sentences) when you need more information before revising. Do NOT include proposal/assessment.
- Return kind="revision" + a brief reply summarizing what you changed, plus a complete proposal + assessment, when you have enough information to apply changes.
- Keep replies short and snappy — the operator is chatting, not reading a report.
- Default to revising when the operator's message is concrete; default to one clarifying question when it's vague.
- When revising: reuse the existing spec's structure and only change what the operator asked for, plus anything logically required by that change.

Data readiness rules:
- PASS only when every core requirement maps to AVAILABLE.
- WARN when requirements map to PARTIAL, or when a non-core nice-to-have is missing.
- BLOCKED when any core signal, entry rule, or exit rule requires missing data.
- When unsure whether data is core, treat it as core.
- Thin theses are allowed. Unavailable data is not allowed.

Requirement-emission rules (avoid false BLOCKEDs):
- One capability per underlying feed. If a signal is derivable from OHLCV (volume, ADV, returns, momentum, moving averages, RSI, realized vol, mean reversion), list `alpaca_equity_ohlcv` (or the relevant OHLCV capability) once. Do NOT emit each derived metric as a separate `assessment.requirements` row.
- When a catalog capability is the proxy for a thesis-mentioned source (e.g. apewisdom_top100 covers WSB / X / Twitter / StockTwits attention), list ONLY the proxy. Do not also list the upstream source names — that produces duplicate MISSING rows for data that's already covered.
- If you must mention a thesis source that the actual spec does not depend on (incidental flavor, replaced by a proxy, deferred to v2), set `core: false` on that requirement OR omit it entirely. The verdict floor treats `core` as true by default; an unset `core` on a MISSING row will escalate to BLOCKED, even if your `notes` say it's non-core.

Idea sleeve: STOCKS
Idea title: Ape Wisdom
Idea thesis: The plan is simple. We start by targeting stocks that are trending in places like r/wallstreetbets and similar circles on X (also monitored by sites like apewisdom.com) and perform technical analysis on hot candidates in the dataset to target which ones still hold an edge and haven’t been fully priced in yet. Some may be large well known companies which we should pay attention to, but I think there’s a lot of gain we can find from the newer stocks on that appear so long as we don’t get greedy. This strategy will consistently sweep apewisdom’s top 100 and filter them out by ones with a high likes to mentions ratio, and those whose current trading volume is within a standard deviation from its average 30 day volume to help weed out ones that have already moved considerably. We can then look at the RSI, several other moving average indicators to fine tune further and then perform market research on the remainders. Also sets reasonable sell limits to enjoy healthy gain without getting greedy.

Current spec (v1, state=DRAFTING):
signal_logic: Primary signal is constructed from the apewisdom_top100 feed. Each daily sweep ranks the top-100 tickers by a composite attention score: (1) likes-to-mentions ratio (upvote ratio from the feed) must exceed a threshold (e.g., top tercile of the current sweep), and (2) current-day trading volume must be within ±1 standard deviation of the ticker's trailing 30-day average daily volume (ADV), computed from OHLCV bars. Tickers passing both filters enter a technical refinement layer: RSI(14) must be between 40 and 65 (not overbought, not in freefall), price must be above its 20-day SMA, and the 9-day EMA must be above the 21-day EMA (short-term momentum confirmation). Survivors of all filters constitute the daily candidate set. Optionally, a manual market-research gate can be applied by the operator before order submission.
entry_rules: Enter long at next-day market open for any ticker that clears all signal filters on the prior day's sweep. Position sizing is equal-weight across all candidates, subject to a maximum of 10 simultaneous open positions. Minimum liquidity gate: ADV must exceed $1M (derived from OHLCV volume). No leverage. No short positions. New entries are only taken if the portfolio has available cash allocation.
exit_rules: Primary profit target: close position at +20% gain from entry price (limit order). Hard stop-loss: close position at -10% from entry price (stop order). Time-based exit: if neither target nor stop is hit within 15 calendar days, exit at market open on day 16. Re-entry into the same ticker is permitted only after a full exit and a minimum 3-day cooling-off period. If a ticker drops out of the apewisdom_top100 entirely for 3 consecutive days while the position is open and is below entry price, exit at next open (sentiment deterioration exit).
universe: Dynamic universe drawn daily from the apewisdom_top100 ranked list, filtered down to U.S. equity tickers tradeable on Alpaca (common stocks and ETFs). Penny stocks below $1.00 are excluded. Tickers with ADV below $1M (30-day) are excluded. No sector or market-cap restriction, though the operator should be aware the universe will skew toward small/mid-cap and meme-adjacent names.
risk_model: Equal-weight position sizing across up to 10 concurrent positions, implying a maximum single-position weight of 10% of portfolio NAV. Hard stop at -10% per position limits maximum single-trade loss to ~1% of NAV. Portfolio-level drawdown circuit breaker: if total portfolio drawdown from peak exceeds 15%, halt new entries until NAV recovers above the 90% of peak threshold. No leverage. Concentration cap: no single ticker may exceed 10% NAV at entry. Sector concentration is not explicitly capped given the opportunistic nature of the universe, but operator review is recommended when >3 positions share a sector.
sweep_params: likes_to_mentions_ratio_threshold: [top-tercile, top-quartile]; rsi_upper_bound: [60, 65, 70]; rsi_lower_bound: [35, 40, 45]; volume_zscore_window: [20, 30]; sma_period: [20, 50]; profit_target_pct: [15, 20, 25]; stop_loss_pct: [8, 10, 12]; max_holding_days: [10, 15, 20]; max_concurrent_positions: [5, 8, 10]
required_data: apewisdom_top100, alpaca_equity_ohlcv
benchmark: SPY (S&P 500 ETF) buy-and-hold over the same period.
acceptance_criteria: min_sharpe=0.8, max_drawdown=25, min_hit_rate=45, other=Average winning trade / average losing trade (profit factor) >= 1.5. Median holding period should fall between 3 and 15 days. At least 50 closed trades required for statistical validity before live deployment.
candidate_strategy_family: Retail-sentiment momentum with technical confirmation (mean-reversion guard)
implementation_notes: apewisdom_top100 coverage begins 2024; backtest window is therefore limited to ~2 years. Operator should acknowledge this constraint and not over-fit on a short sample. The manual market-research gate described in the thesis is supported as an optional operator review step before order submission but cannot be automated or backtested. Sentiment deterioration exit requires daily re-scoring of open positions against the apewisdom feed — ensure the pipeline re-runs the sweep for held tickers even when no new entries are triggered. ADV and volume z-score are derived from alpaca_equity_ohlcv; no separate data feed is needed.

Data capability catalog (2026-04-30.2):
- alpaca_equity_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; AVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=2016-present notes=Daily + intraday bars via Alpaca. Wired into the active strategy bank and the Lab pipeline. The default price feed for equities-sleeve research. Covers any signal derivable from OHLCV bars: open/high/low/close, volume, average daily volume (ADV), trailing returns, momentum, moving averages, RSI, realized volatility, mean reversion bands. Strategies should list this capability_id once for the underlying feed and not emit each derived metric as a separate data requirement.
- alpaca_crypto_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; PARTIAL; sleeves=CRYPTO; coverage=2024-10-23-present notes=BTC and ETH only via Alpaca. ~1.5 years of history — sufficient for execution and recent-regime work, insufficient for multi-era analysis. Use ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv for longer history (research-only, no execution coupling).
- ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv: Price OHLCV; PARTIAL; sleeves=CRYPTO; coverage=2017-present (BTC); shorter for alts notes=Public Binance OHLCV pulled via ccxt for research backtests only. NOT wired into the executor — execution stays on Alpaca. Operator-pulled: no live connector, scripted backfills.
- alpaca_options_chain: Options chain; PARTIAL; sleeves=OPTIONS; coverage=live snapshot only notes=Live chain via Alpaca. NOT historical: no point-in-time reconstruction for backtests. Strategies that depend on historical chain state need a separate provider before they can be Lab-tested.
- iv_surface_constructed: Implied vol surface; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=OPTIONS; notes=Would require historical chain history we don't have. No connector and no construction pipeline. Strategies depending on IV surface skew/term structure should BLOCK.
- wikipedia_pageviews: Attention proxies; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; coverage=2016-present (research backfills) notes=Wikimedia REST API is freely accessible; research backfill scripts exist. No production connector wired into the Lab pipeline yet — operator-driven pulls. Don't claim AVAILABLE until a daily-update connector lands.
- gdelt_full: Attention proxies; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; coverage=2015-present (research backfills) notes=GDELT API is free; backfill scripts exist. No daily-update connector wired into the Lab. Same status caveat as wikipedia_pageviews.
- google_trends: Attention proxies; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO; notes=No wired connector. Sample-rate noise + rate limiting on the public endpoint make it a v2-or-later candidate.
- apewisdom_top100: Sentiment; AVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=2024-present (continuous) notes=Top-100 retail-attention rank with mention/upvote ratios. Wired as the canonical retail-sentiment feed for stocks. THIS IS THE PROXY for r/wallstreetbets, X/Twitter trending tickers, StockTwits, Reddit investing subs, and adjacent retail communities — Ape Wisdom aggregates these into one feed. If a thesis mentions 'WSB / Reddit / X / Twitter / StockTwits / retail buzz / social trending' as a signal, list apewisdom_top100 once and do NOT emit those underlying source names as separate requirements. Raw per-thread velocity is not exposed; only the aggregate rank + mention ratio.
- reddit_wsb_velocity: Sentiment; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS; notes=Raw r/wallstreetbets per-thread velocity is NOT pulled separately. Use apewisdom_top100 for the aggregated signal. Strategies that genuinely require sub-Ape-Wisdom granularity should BLOCK.
- fundamentals_basic: Fundamentals; PARTIAL; sleeves=STOCKS; coverage=current snapshot only notes=Market cap + average daily volume reachable via Alpaca asset metadata. Not point-in-time. Strategies needing historical fundamentals (P/E, EPS surprise, balance-sheet items) are NOT covered.
- crypto_onchain: Crypto on-chain; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=CRYPTO; notes=No on-chain connector (Glassnode, Dune, etc.). Strategies referencing whale flows, exchange reserves, miner behavior, or staking metrics should BLOCK.
- macro_fred_subset: Macro; UNAVAILABLE; sleeves=STOCKS, CRYPTO, OPTIONS; notes=FRED series are operator-curated as needed for one-off research; no Lab-wired connector. Strategies depending on macro inputs (fed funds path, CPI surprises, yield curve shape) should BLOCK until a connector lands.

raw_proposal.signal_logic:
Primary signal is constructed from the apewisdom_top100 feed. Each daily sweep ranks the top-100 tickers by a composite attention score: (1) likes-to-mentions ratio (upvote ratio from the feed) must exceed a threshold (e.g., top tercile of the current sweep), and (2) current-day trading volume must be within ±1 standard deviation of the ticker's trailing 30-day average daily volume (ADV), computed from OHLCV bars. Tickers passing both filters enter a technical refinement layer: RSI(14) must be between 40 and 65 (not overbought, not in freefall), price must be above its 20-day SMA, and the 9-day EMA must be above the 21-day EMA (short-term momentum confirmation). Survivors of all filters constitute the daily candidate set.

Conviction Score: Each surviving ticker is assigned a continuous conviction score C ∈ [0, 1] composed of three sub-signals, equally weighted by default (weights are sweep parameters):
  - Sentiment strength (0–1): normalized likes-to-mentions ratio percentile rank within the current sweep's top-100.
  - Technical alignment (0–1): composite of RSI proximity to 50 (peak score at RSI=52), EMA spread magnitude (9-EMA minus 21-EMA as % of price), and SMA distance (price % above 20-day SMA).
  - Earnings proximity modifier: if an earnings announcement is within 5 calendar days (before or after entry), apply a dampening multiplier of 0.6 to C to reflect two-way event risk. If earnings are 6–15 days out, apply a mild dampening of 0.85. Beyond 15 days, no adjustment.

Optionally, a manual market-research gate can be applied by the operator before order submission.
raw_proposal.entry_rules: Enter long at next-day market open for any ticker that clears all signal filters on the prior day's sweep. Position sizing is equal-weight across all candidates, subject to a maximum of 10 simultaneous open positions. Minimum liquidity gate: ADV must exceed $1M (derived from OHLCV volume). No leverage. No short positions. New entries are only taken if the portfolio has available cash allocation. The conviction score C is computed at entry and stored with the position record; it is used to set dynamic exit targets (see exit rules).
raw_proposal.exit_rules:
Exit targets are dynamically set at entry using the conviction score C ∈ [0, 1]:
  - Profit target: PT = 12% + (C × 16%), yielding a range of +12% (low conviction) to +28% (high conviction). Implemented as a limit order placed at entry.
  - Stop-loss: SL = 7% + (C × 5%), yielding a range of -7% (low conviction) to -12% (high conviction). Higher conviction justifies a slightly wider stop to avoid shaking out on noise. Implemented as a stop order placed at entry.
  - Both orders are placed as a bracket at entry and updated if C is recalculated on a subsequent day (e.g., if the operator re-runs the sweep mid-hold).

Time-based exit: if neither target nor stop is hit within 15 calendar days, exit at market open on day 16.

Sentiment deterioration exit: if a ticker drops out of the apewisdom_top100 entirely for 3 consecutive days while the position is open and is below entry price, exit at next open.

Re-entry into the same ticker is permitted only after a full exit and a minimum 3-day cooling-off period.
raw_proposal.risk_model: Equal-weight position sizing across up to 10 concurrent positions, implying a maximum single-position weight of 10% of portfolio NAV. Dynamic stop-loss ranges from -7% to -12% per position depending on conviction score, limiting maximum single-trade loss to approximately 0.7%–1.2% of NAV. Portfolio-level drawdown circuit breaker: if total portfolio drawdown from peak exceeds 15%, halt new entries until NAV recovers above 90% of peak threshold. No leverage. Concentration cap: no single ticker may exceed 10% NAV at entry. Sector concentration is not explicitly capped given the opportunistic nature of the universe, but operator review is recommended when >3 positions share a sector.
raw_proposal.universe: Dynamic universe drawn daily from the apewisdom_top100 ranked list, filtered down to U.S. equity tickers tradeable on Alpaca (common stocks and ETFs). Penny stocks below $1.00 are excluded. Tickers with ADV below $1M (30-day) are excluded. No sector or market-cap restriction, though the operator should be aware the universe will skew toward small/mid-cap and meme-adjacent names.
raw_proposal.benchmark: SPY (S&P 500 ETF) buy-and-hold over the same period.

acceptance_criteria.other: Average winning trade / average losing trade (profit factor) >= 1.5. Median holding period should fall between 3 and 15 days. At least 50 closed trades required for statistical validity before live deployment. Conviction score calibration check: high-conviction trades (C > 0.7) should show a higher average return than low-conviction trades (C < 0.3) in backtest — if not, the conviction weighting should be re-tuned before live deployment.

raw_proposal.candidate_strategy_family: Retail-sentiment momentum with technical confirmation and conviction-scaled dynamic exits

Operator should acknowledge this constraint and not over-fit on a short sample. The manual market-research gate described in the thesis is supported as an optional operator review step before order submission but cannot be automated or backtested. Sentiment deterioration exit requires daily re-scoring of open positions against the apewisdom feed — ensure the pipeline re-runs the sweep for held tickers even when no new entries are triggered. ADV, volume z-score, RSI, SMA, and EMA are all derived from alpaca_equity_ohlcv; no separate data feed is needed. Earnings dates must be sourced for the earnings proximity modifier — Alpaca asset metadata does not provide point-in-time earnings calendars; operator should wire a free earnings calendar source (e.g., Nasdaq earnings calendar, EOD Historical Data free tier) or apply the dampener manually during the operator review gate. Headline sentiment is a v2 candidate: free APIs exist (e.g., NewsAPI free tier, Alpaca news feed) but none are currently wired into the Lab pipeline. Flag for future integration when a connector lands.

requirements.notes: Canonical retail-sentiment feed. Covers WSB, X, Reddit, StockTwits aggregation. Wired and continuous from 2024.
Covers all OHLCV-derived signals: RSI, SMA, EMA, ADV, volume z-score, returns. One entry for the underlying feed.

Required for the earnings proximity conviction modifier. Not wired into the Lab. Marked non-core because the dampener can be applied manually via the operator review gate in v1; automated use would require a connector. Flag as v2.

raw_assessment.suggested_action: Wire a free earnings calendar feed (e.g., Nasdaq earnings calendar or Alpaca news metadata) to automate the conviction dampener. Until then, apply earnings proximity adjustment manually during the operator review gate.
warnings: Earnings calendar is not wired into the Lab — the earnings proximity conviction modifier must be applied manually or skipped until a connector is available.
Backtest window is limited to ~2 years (apewisdom_top100 coverage from 2024). Avoid over-fitting on a short sample.
Headline sentiment remains a manual gate in v1; no automated news NLP feed is available in the Lab yet.
Conviction score calibration (high-C vs low-C return differential) should be validated in backtest before live deployment — if the signal shows no predictive lift, revert to fixed exits.
